user.title: Configure prediction settings
user.description: Configure how the predictor works. Warning: changing certain settings may require relearning previously learned patterns.

data_description.time_bucket_size_in_minutes: Defines the time resolution for learning patterns. Smaller values (15–30 minutes) capture fast changes in detail. Larger values (120–240 minutes) produce more stable, generalized patterns.
data_description.use_day_of_week: Allows the system to model different behavior across weekdays and weekends.
data_description.use_month_of_year: Enables learning of recurring monthly patterns (e.g., heating season, summer watering).
data_description.use_season: Enables modeling of seasonal behavioral patterns.
data_description.calendar_features: Select calendar entities to consider special days (e.g., holidays, events, vacation days).
data_description.advanced_configuration: Show additional settings for fine-tuning prediction learning and adaptation.

advanced_configuration.title: Advanced settings
advanced_configuration.description: Fine-tune learning speed, adaptability, and model stability.

data_description.preset:
Choose a behavior profile:

- Stable: For devices with consistent behavior patterns (e.g., TV, lighting, heating).
- Moderate: For moderately variable usage patterns (e.g., household appliances).
- Dynamic: For highly variable behavior (e.g., presence detection, manual control).
- Custom: Manually configure all parameters.
data_description.enable_adaptive_half_life: Enables adaptive learning speed by adjusting the learning half-life based on pattern stability. Stable patterns are remembered longer, while unstable patterns are adapted faster.
data_description.adaptive_prune_interval: Controls model complexity adaptively. Stable patterns are pruned less frequently, while highly variable patterns trigger more frequent cleanup.
data_description.adaptive_persistence: Enables adaptive state transition stability. The system learns that some states are naturally more persistent than others and adjusts transition probabilities accordingly.

data_description.half_life_hours: Base learning half-life. Controls how quickly old observations lose weight in exponential smoothing. Smaller values enable faster adaptation, larger values create more stable models.
data_description.short_term_error_half_life_factor: Decay rate of short-term prediction errors. Controls how quickly the model reacts to recent prediction deviations.
data_description.long_term_error_half_life_factor: Decay rate of long-term error memory, providing stable reference behavior against short-term fluctuations.
data_description.base_state_inertia_strength: Base influence of current state persistence when calculating predictions.
data_description.persistence_half_life_factor: Defines the half-life of learned state duration statistics. The effective half-life is calculated as base_half_life × persistence_half_life_factor. Smaller values make the model adapt faster to changes in typical state durations. Larger values make state persistence more stable over time.
data_description.fast_baseline_half_life_factor: Half-life factor for fast baseline tracking to detect short-term behavior changes.
data_description.slow_baseline_half_life_factor: Half-life factor for slow baseline tracking to capture long-term behavioral trends.
data_description.min_prune_interval_factor: Controls how frequently rarely used patterns are removed. Higher values mean less frequent pruning.
data_description.adaptive_tau: Enables automatic tuning of drift thresholds based on pattern stability.
data_description.tau_enter: Upper drift detection threshold. If deviation between fast and slow baselines exceeds this value, a new behavioral regime is detected.
data_description.tau_exit: Lower drift threshold. The system returns to normal mode when deviation falls below this value. Must be lower than tau_enter to ensure hysteresis.
data_description.background_decay_half_life_factor: Multiplier for background decay applied to all keys, including dormant ones. A value of 0 disables background decay entirely (default). A positive value causes all stored patterns — even rarely observed ones — to slowly decay over time. The effective decay half-life is calculated as background_decay_half_life_factor × base_half_life. Use a small positive value (e.g. 5–20) to gradually forget stale patterns that are no longer being observed.

init.title: Time pattern settings
init.description: Warning: changing these settings may require relearning patterns from scratch.

data_description.calendar_features: Consider special days using calendar entities.

confirm_reset.title: ⚠️ Confirm pattern reset
confirm_reset.description:
Changing time pattern configuration will erase previously learned statistical models.

The system will rebuild models using new incoming data, which may take several days or weeks.

Are you sure you want to continue?

data.confirm: Yes, delete learned patterns and apply new settings
